app.title: FlintWave Flash
app.about_blurb:
Flash .kdhx firmware to BTECH, Baofeng, Radtel,
and other KDH bootloader radios from any OS.
tooltip.refresh: Re-scan serial ports and probe each one for a radio in bootloader mode.
statusbar.update_tooltip:
Currently running v{local} (latest available: {remote}).
Click to open the releases page in your browser.
lang.download_failed: Couldn't download the {language} translation. Staying on the previous language.

dialog.report.title: Submit Test Report
dialog.report.success:
Flash completed successfully!
Would you like to submit a test report?
dialog.report.failure:
Flash failed.
Would you like to submit a report to help us debug?
dialog.report.email_hint: You can also email reports to flintwave@tuta.com
dialog.report.subject: Test Report: {radio} — {status}
dialog.error_no_handset_flash:
Check at least one handset in the Handset column.
Click 'Refresh / Probe' if your cable isn't listed.
dialog.error_no_handset_diag:
Check at least one handset in the Handset column to run diagnostics on it.
Click 'Refresh / Probe' to re-scan.
dialog.untested_body:
{radio} has NOT been tested with this tool.

The protocol should be compatible, but flashing untested
firmware could potentially brick the radio.

Download anyway?
dialog.untested_warning:
NOTE: {radio} has NOT been tested with this tool.
The protocol should be compatible, but proceed with caution.
dialog.same_version_body:
You already flashed v{version} to this radio.

Flash the same version again?
dialog.older_version_body:
This firmware (v{file_version}) is older than what was
last flashed (v{last_version}).

Flash an older version?
dialog.confirm_single:
{warning}Make sure the {radio} on {port} is in bootloader mode:

1. Power off the radio
2. Hold {keys}
3. Turn power knob to turn on
4. Screen stays blank, green LED lights up

Do not disconnect the radio or cable during the update!

Ready to flash?
dialog.confirm_batch_body:
{warning}Flash {count} handsets sequentially:
  {ports}

Each {radio} must be in bootloader mode:
  • Power off, hold {keys}, then turn the power knob on
  • Screen stays blank, green Rx LED lights up

Do not disconnect any cable during the batch!

Ready to start?
dialog.cleanup_body:
Downloaded firmware files are using {size_mb:.1f} MB:
{path}

Delete them to free up space?
(You can always re-download later)
fallback.bootloader_keys: the bootloader keys (check your radio's manual)
log.firmware_ready: Firmware ready. You can now flash it.
log.power_cycle: Power cycle the radio and check Menu > Radio Info.
log.fw_is_latest: Firmware v{version} is the latest available.
log.fw_newer_available: Note: v{latest} is available (you flashed v{current}).
log.may_need_power_cycle: Radio may need to be power cycled and put back in bootloader mode.
log.batch_abort_permission: Aborting batch — fix the permission and retry.
log.dryrun_header: *** DRY RUN MODE — no serial communication ***
log.fail_too_large: FAIL: File too large ({size} bytes, max {max})
log.dryrun_passed: DRY RUN PASSED — firmware file is valid and ready to flash
log.crc_fail_chunk: FAIL: CRC self-check failed on chunk {chunk}
log.diag_responding: Radio is responding! Flash should work.
log.diag_check: Check: cable, bootloader mode, serial port.
log.dialout_intro: This is a Linux serial-port permission issue, not a flashing problem.
log.dialout_user: Your user is not in the 'dialout' group, so it can't open {port}.
log.dialout_fix_intro: Fix it once and you won't see this again:
log.dialout_relogin: Then log out and back in (a full re-login, not just a new terminal).
hint.no_firmware.title: Step 1 — Get firmware
hint.no_firmware.body: Choose a radio model in the Firmware column, then click “Download Latest” if available, or “Browse…” to pick a .kdhx file you already downloaded.
hint.no_handset.title: Step 2 — Connect a handset
hint.no_handset.body: Plug in your programming cable (PC03 or compatible K1 cable). Detected ports appear in the Handset column; PC03 cables are auto-checked. Click “Refresh / Probe” after plugging in a new cable to re-scan and check which ports respond like a radio in bootloader mode.
hint.batch_ready.title: Step 4 — Batch flash
hint.batch_ready.body: Multiple handsets are checked. Clicking “Flash Firmware” will flash the same firmware to each one sequentially. Make sure every selected radio is in bootloader mode (green Rx LED on) before starting.
hint.ready_dryrun.title: Step 3 — Verify the firmware
hint.ready_dryrun.body: Click “Dry Run” to validate the firmware file and confirm the packets build without serial communication. Optionally click “Diagnostics” to test the cable.
hint.ready_flash.title: Step 4 — Flash
hint.ready_flash.body:
Put the radio in bootloader mode:
  1. Power the radio off completely
  2. Hold the bootloader keys (shown in the radio info)
  3. While holding, turn the power knob to turn on
  4. Screen stays blank, green Rx LED lights up
  5. Do NOT release the keys until the LED is on

Then click “Flash Firmware”. Do not unplug the cable or turn off the radio during the flash.
hint.downloading.title: Downloading firmware…
hint.downloading.body: Fetching the latest firmware. The progress bar tracks the download. Once finished, the file will be filled in automatically.
hint.flashing.title: Flashing in progress…
hint.flashing.body: Streaming firmware to the radio. Do NOT unplug the cable, power off the radio, or close this window until the operation completes.
hint.dryrun.title: Dry run in progress…
hint.dryrun.body: Validating the firmware file and verifying packet CRCs. No serial communication is happening.
hint.diagnostics.title: Diagnostics in progress…
hint.diagnostics.body: Sending a handshake to the cable to confirm connectivity. If the radio responds, flashing should work.
hint.complete.title: Flash complete!
hint.complete.body: Power cycle the radio and check Menu › Radio Info to confirm the new version. See the log on the right for full details.
hint.dryrun_complete.title: Dry run passed
hint.dryrun_complete.body: The firmware file is structurally valid and every packet's CRC verified. No data was sent to a radio. Put your radio in bootloader mode and click Flash Firmware when you're ready.
hint.diag_complete.title: Diagnostics passed
hint.diag_complete.body: The selected handset answered the bootloader handshake — the cable, port, and bootloader-mode setup are all working. You can proceed to flash.
hint.failed.title: Operation failed
hint.failed.body: The last operation did not complete successfully. Read the log on the right for the error message. The radio may need to be power cycled and put back in bootloader mode before trying again.
